guide.title: Reference Manual
guide.subtitle: A comprehensive, page-by-page, button-by-button manual of every capability within Gensui Command Center.
guide.sec_dashboard_desc: Real-time fleet monitoring and security status at a glance.
guide.sec_fleet_desc: View, search, and filter all enrolled Shogun instances.
guide.sec_detail_desc: Deep-dive into a single fleet member's status, tools, and security configuration.
guide.sec_network_desc: Interactive SVG visualization of your entire Shogun fleet and network environment.
guide.sec_groups_desc: Organize Shogun instances into logical groups for collective management.
guide.sec_postures_desc: Define and manage what Shogun instances are allowed to do.
guide.sec_harakiri_desc: Emergency kill-switches with multi-mode shutdown capabilities.
guide.sec_activity_desc: Real-time telemetry feed from all connected Shogun instances.
guide.sec_audit_desc: Tamper-proof HMAC-chained event log for compliance and forensics.
guide.sec_alerts_desc: Security alerts and threshold notifications from the fleet.
guide.sec_enrollment_desc: Manage enrollment tokens and approve/reject pending Shogun instances.
guide.sec_settings_desc: Profile management, password changes, and admin user management.
guide.sec_security_desc: Comprehensive runtime security architecture protecting every Shogun instance in the fleet.
guide.sec_fleet_audit_desc: Multi-instance audit analytics, compliance reporting, and HMAC chain verification.
guide.sec_identity_desc: SPIFFE/OIDC SSO, service accounts, and API key management.
guide.card_fleet_stats_desc: Four metric cards at the top. Each shows a count and optional total:
guide.card_members_online_desc: Shows how many Shogun instances are currently connected vs. total enrolled.
guide.card_active_samurai_desc: Total sub-agents running across all fleet members.
guide.card_mado_sessions_desc: Active browser automation sessions across all Shoguns.
guide.card_alert_row_desc: Three clickable cards below the stats grid:
guide.card_active_alerts_desc: Number of unresolved alerts. Turns red when critical alerts exist. Click to navigate to the Alerts page.
guide.card_harakiri_active_desc: Shows "None" (green) or the count of active Harakiri events (red pulsing). Click to go to Harakiri Control.
guide.card_pending_enrollment_desc: Number of Shogun instances awaiting approval. Click to go to Enrollment Management.
guide.card_posture_banner_desc: When a Global Posture Override is active (see Postures), a red danger-glowing banner appears showing the posture name, who activated it, and the reason. This means all fleet members are currently locked to a specific security posture regardless of their individual settings.
guide.card_auto_refresh_desc: All dashboard data auto-refreshes every 10 seconds. No manual reload needed.
guide.card_search_bar_desc: A text search field and a status dropdown at the top. Search by instance name or hostname. Filter by status: All, Online, or Offline.
guide.card_fleet_table_desc: A sortable table with columns: Instance (name + hostname, clickable to detail), Status (online/offline badge with pulse), Environment, Harakiri (active state or "—"), Samurai count, Workflows count, Last Seen timestamp. Data auto-refreshes every 10 seconds.
guide.security_architecture_desc: Shogun's security architecture is built in 6 layered phases, each independently enforceable.
guide.security_toolgate_desc: ToolGate is the core security enforcement engine in Shogun. Every tool call passes through ToolGate before execution.
guide.security_quarantine_desc: The Quarantine system replaces hard deletes with recoverable soft deletes.
guide.security_injection_desc: External content can contain adversarial instructions designed to hijack the AI agent.
guide.security_posture_push_desc: When a Gensui administrator modifies a security posture, the updated configuration is pushed to all connected Shogun instances.
guide.security_fleet_audit_desc: Multi-instance audit dashboard providing fleet-wide visibility.
guide.security_enterprise_id_desc: SPIFFE/SPIRE trust domains, Keycloak/OIDC SSO, and service accounts.
guide.security_immutable_chain_desc: All security events are dual-written to Layer 1 (operational) and Layer 2 (immutable, 7-year retention).
guide.security_postures_detail_desc: Security postures define the complete permission profile for a Shogun instance.
